hypothesis: PR #1758 seed (CaseOps + PreQuant TTT LR=1e-3 Unfrozen, 3-seed claim)

Lower muon_momentum_warmup_start from 0.92 to 0.85, matching the canonical modded-nanogpt recipe; keeps final momentum 0.99 and 0.33 warmup duration intact. Mechanism: early high momentum (0.92) over-smooths the informative early-training gradient signal during the high-LR phase; starting at 0.85 lets the model adapt faster in the first ~10% of the budget while still saturating to 0.99 well before warmdown.

Split Muon weight decay into attn (0.095) vs MLP (0.115) param groups, targeting stronger L2 regularization on MLP matrices which have 2× more parameters per block and are more prone to overfitting in short training runs.

Replace cosine LR decay across TTT chunks with constant LR (h.ttt_lr=0.005 throughout), eliminating near-zero adaptation in later chunks which the cosine schedule reduces to ~0.001% of full LR by chunk 90/122.

Switch TTT optimizer from SGD (lr=0.005, momentum=0.9) to AdamW (lr=0.001, betas=(0.9,0.999), wd=0) to leverage adaptive gradient normalization in the ~3-step-per-chunk regime where Adam's second-moment immediately normalizes gradient scale without needing warmup steps.

Add per-head sigmoid output gate to attention (init logit(0.95)≈2.944 for near-identity start), placed after XSA before reshape — lightweight form of NeurIPS 2025 "Gated Attention" (arXiv:2505.06708, also adopted in Qwen3-Next), introducing a bounded non-linearity on the SDPA output.

Add learnable per-feature embedding scale (embed_scale, shape=[embedding_dim], init=ones) applied after the input RMSNorm, enabling the model to learn asymmetric weighting between the input and output paths of the tied embedding matrix, with embed_scale also propagating through all x0 residual injections across blocks.
Split Muon weight decay into MLP (0.115) vs attention (0.095) param groups, matching the SOTA stack doc's muon_wd_mlp spec that was not yet implemented in code.

Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration (top-100 tokens get sqrt(2) activation boost → 2× H weight, biasing quantization error toward Zipfian-dominant tokens) plus reduce block_size 128→64 for finer within-block error propagation.

Within-shard stratification: divide each shard into 4 positional strata (beginning/quarter/half/end), shuffle within each stratum, then interleave so consecutive pops alternate across strata, ensuring ~10-20% of any shard's sequences drawn during training come evenly from all shard positions rather than clustering in one region.

Add z-loss auxiliary penalty (1e-4 * logsumexp(logits)^2) to training forward pass; prevents log-partition-function drift complementary to the existing logit_softcap, improving calibration without touching forward_logits or eval paths.
Split Muon weight decay into attn (0.095) vs MLP (0.115) param groups — resubmit after eval_budget_overrun by environment jitter (3.7s over); training-only change adds no eval time.
Shorten Muon momentum warmup fraction from 0.33 → 0.22 to spend ~11% more wallclock at full 0.99 momentum (matching canonical modded-nanogpt fast-ramp schedules).

Switch TTT optimizer from SGD (lr=0.005, 3 epochs) to AdamW (lr=0.001, 2 epochs): fewer but higher-quality gradient steps via adaptive normalization, fitting within the eval budget (first attempt with 3 AdamW epochs overran by 3.8s).

FreqGPTQ: two-pass calibration — fast data-only pass counts token frequencies (no model inference), then Hessian collection pass applies sqrt(2) boost to top-100 frequent token activations before H=X^TX; plus block_size 128→64 for finer error propagation.
Bifurcate per-head q_gain into [H,2] — col-0 scales RoPE-rotated head dims, col-1 scales NoPE region — letting the optimizer pick separate temperatures for the positional vs content-only sub-spaces.

Replace fixed Muon NS coefficients (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) with Polar Express per-iteration minimax-optimized tuples for 5 steps, and set min_lr floor to 0.10 to prevent LR collapsing to zero at end of warmdown.

Add a per-feature bigram mixing channel to the embedding: a zero-init [dim] parameter (bigram_scale) that blends the previous token's RMSNorm-normalized embedding into the current token's embedding, giving the model immediate access to bigram context at the input layer.

Add ResFormer-style value residual: cache layer-0 V once, mix `v_mix[0]·V + v_mix[1]·V_layer0` into every later attention's V via a learnable 2-scalar per-block param (init [1.0, 0.0] for step-0 identity); structurally orthogonal to existing XSA / h_gate / bifurcated QK-gain / 3-layer recurrence.
Add gentle monotone position-weighted CE: linear ramp from weight 0.8 (pos=0) to 1.2 (pos=T-1), mean-normalized to 1.0, to up-weight later-context predictions and strengthen long-range modeling.

TTT per-type LR groups (output proj 3x, input proj 0.5x, others 1x) + momentum 0.95: concentrates adaptation budget on quantized output projections closest to the loss, backed by PR #995 ablation showing this pattern improves short-horizon TTT adaptation.

Replace non-overlapping stride (seq_len=2048) with 50% overlapping stride (stride=1024) in shard sampling: 2x more unique starting positions per shard, each token seen from two distinct left-context windows, reducing train/eval stride mismatch and providing richer gradient signal per token within the 600s budget.
Split Muon WD (attn=0.095, MLP=0.115) plus reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→48 to clear the systematic ~603s eval overrun seen in this environment.

Raise EMA_DECAY 0.9965→0.998 to extend the averaging half-life from ~198 to ~346 steps, capturing more of the mid-late warmdown trajectory where weights are settling under decreasing LR, reducing per-step noise.
Replace binary top-100 sqrt(2) Hessian boost with continuous sqrt(freq/mean) weighting clamped [0.25,4.0], and reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32 for finer per-block error compensation.

Remove cosine-over-chunks LR decay in TTT: use constant LR=0.005 throughout all chunks instead of decaying to near-zero for later chunks, giving equal adaptation budget to all non-last chunks including the critical tail of the validation set.
Polar Express NS coefficients + MIN_LR=0.10 floor (same as exp_016) with ttt_epochs reduced 3→2 to fit within 600s eval budget.

Re-submit ResFormer-style value residual with layer-0 v_mix nullified via register_parameter('v_mix', None) so DDP find_unused_parameters=False (default) is happy — layer 0 captures v_first but never consumes one, so its mix param has no gradient path.

TTT per-type LR groups (3 groups: out_proj 3x, in_proj 0.5x, others 1x) + momentum 0.95 — same technique as prev submit but params batched into 3 groups (not 136) to eliminate per-param overhead that caused 3.5s eval overrun.

Add normalized focal loss (γ=0.5) on top of position ramp: downweights easy tokens (high p_correct), upweights hard tokens, normalized to mean=1 to preserve gradient scale, addressing token-difficulty bias orthogonally to the positional ramp.

Sequence-length warmup curriculum: train at seq_len=512 for first 30% of wallclock (faster steps due to O(L²) attention savings, ~10-20% more gradient steps), then switch to seq_len=2048 for the remainder; both graph shapes pre-compiled during warmup to avoid in-budget recompile overhead.

Add a learned per-feature word-boundary bias (word_boundary_scale, zero-init [D]) applied only to SP8192 word-start tokens (▁ prefix), injecting explicit morphological structure into the embedding pipeline that the model would otherwise need to infer from token IDs.

Add stochastic depth regularization with linear schedule (drop_path_prob linearly from 0% at layer 0 to 10% at layer 10), applied per-sample during training only, to provide implicit ensemble diversity and faster gradient flow through shorter effective paths.
Replace binary top-100 sqrt(2) Hessian boost with continuous sqrt(freq/mean) weighting clamped [0.25,4.0]; block_size stays at 64 to avoid eval budget overrun.

Move enable_looping_at 0.35 → 0.22 so the looped (eval-matched) depth-recurrence architecture gets ~6% of high-LR training before warmdown starts at frac=0.28, instead of activating only after warmdown has begun decaying the LR.

Constant LR throughout TTT chunks (remove cosine decay) with 2 SGD epochs: uniform adaptation budget across all chunks instead of decaying to zero for late chunks, with epoch reduction to fit eval budget (baseline SGD/3ep cosine is right at the 600s edge).

Lower min_lr from 0.10 to 0.02: eliminates the 43s flat-LR tail at end of warmdown (where LR was clamped at 10% of peak), enabling deeper final convergence aligned with linear-decay-to-zero (D2Z) research showing this schedule is optimal.

Add label smoothing ε=0.1 to CE: prevents overconfidence on easy tokens without redistributing gradient between positions, complementary to the existing z-loss (logit-scale) and position ramp (positional-bias) regularization.

Apply cosine LR decay across TTT epochs within each chunk: epoch 0 gets full LR, epoch 1 gets 0.75×, epoch 2 gets 0.25×, preventing SGD momentum overshoot in later epochs and yielding a more stably converged adapted model per chunk.

Add learned per-feature word-boundary bias (word_boundary_scale, zero-init [D]) for SP8192 word-start tokens; LUT populated from val_data.sp in train_model() so deserialize() adds no tokenizer load overhead.

Add RWKV-style per-channel learnable token-shift (smear) that blends the previous-position hidden state into each block's pre-attention input via a sigmoid gate, init logit -3.0 (~5% prev-token), giving attention free position-1 context that complements partial-RoPE.

Mask the first 25% of each training sequence (positions 0 to seq_len//4-1) from the cross-entropy loss, focusing gradient signal on positions with ≥512 tokens of context and reducing high-entropy zero-context gradient noise.

Add 2% random token replacement noise to training inputs (token-level augmentation), forcing the model to use broader context rather than local token copying, complementing existing stochastic depth regularization without reducing gradient signal.

Lower enable_looping_at 0.22→0.15: engage 3-layer depth recurrence ~42s earlier so looped layers receive ~9% more wallclock at full effective depth, trading ~10% raw step count for richer training of the looped circuit.

Increase ttt_epochs 2→4 with cosine LR decay within each chunk's adaptation (lr anneals from ttt_lr at epoch 0 to ~15% of ttt_lr at epoch 3), restoring the SOTA-stack epoch count and preventing oscillation in later passes.

Merge GPTQ pre-pass into Hessian collection (one data load instead of two), use continuous sqrt(freq/mean) Hessian weighting clamped [0.25,4.0], and reduce calibration batches 64→48 to clear the systematic 3.4s eval budget overrun.
Replace linear warmdown with cosine warmdown: LR follows 0.5*(1+cos(π*t)) shape during warmdown phase, spending more time near peak LR early and dropping steeply near the end for better convergence.

Remove label smoothing (ε=0.1→0.0): z-loss + logit softcap already prevent overconfidence, and uniform smoothing is anti-signal in the underfitting regime by pulling toward a uniform distribution that contradicts the real token distribution.

Add within-word subword-position encoding: two new per-feature scales (sub1_scale for 2nd-in-word tokens, sub2_scale for 3rd+-in-word tokens) extending the existing word_boundary_scale to a full 3-class BPE position signal, zero-initialized with 1024 extra parameters.

Replace per-block attn_norm/mlp_norm RMSNorm with Dynamic Tanh (DyT, arXiv:2503.10622): tanh(alpha*x) with learnable scalar alpha (init 1.0), reusing existing post-sublayer attn_scale/mlp_scale as the affine gamma; final_norm and inline q/k/embed F.rms_norm unchanged.

Add Polyak EMA weight averaging (decay=0.998) during TTT scoring: before each chunk's score-first pass, swap in the EMA-averaged weights; restore training weights afterward; update EMA after every gradient step. Stable averaged weights reduce gradient-noise artifacts in TTT predictions.
ttt_epochs 2→3 with cosine LR decay within chunk (full ttt_lr at epoch 0, 75% at epoch 1, 25% at epoch 2), giving one more adaptation pass than baseline while fitting in the 600s eval budget.
Reduce prefix loss-masking from seq_len//4 (512 tokens) to seq_len//8 (256 tokens), giving 17% more gradient positions per sequence to exploit in our gradient-starved 600s training budget.
Lower enable_looping_at from 0.15 to 0.08 — more training time under the eval-active looped architecture (eval always uses looping=True), better train/eval architectural match.

Add targeted MLP activation dropout (p=0.05) to the three looped layers (3–5) only: these layers receive ~3× gradient signal when depth recurrence is active (frac≥0.35), so per-layer dropout at the qualitative 0→nonzero threshold should force redundant representations and improve post-quant robustness without significantly slowing convergence in the other 8 layers.

Differentiate GPTQ clip sigmas by layer type: attention layers use attn_clip_sigmas=10.0 (tighter, finer quantization grid for concentrated weight distributions) while MLP layers keep matrix_clip_sigmas=12.85 (wider, preserving heavy-tailed outliers).

Replace cosine warmdown with linear decay and lower min_lr from 0.02 to 0.004, giving a WSD-style trapezoidal schedule that decays uniformly to near-zero rather than flatlining at 2% of peak LR for the last ~15% of warmdown.
Add focal loss modulation (gamma=0.5) with detached weights and mean-normalization to redirect gradient from easy/common tokens toward hard/rare tokens in the underfitting regime.

Add a zero-init trigram channel (per-feature [dim] scale over the 2-prev-token normalized embedding) extending the existing bigram mechanism, giving the embedding pipeline a 2-hop causal look-back before the first transformer block.

Add Canon-C (causal depthwise conv1d kernel=4 with residual, zero-init) before each block's MLP — Allen-Zhu, NeurIPS 2025 (arXiv:2512.17351); generalises short-conv operations from Mamba/GLA/Primer into a universal pre-MLP horizontal-mixing layer, complementing the existing kernel-2 shift_mix at the attention path.

Polyak EMA (decay=0.998) for TTT chunk scoring using pre-allocated swap buffers: eliminates per-chunk GPU memory allocation (clone→copy_), cutting overhead from ~10s to <2s, while preserving the stable-weights-for-scoring benefit.

Keep ttt_epochs=2 but add cosine LR decay within epochs (epoch 0 at full ttt_lr=0.005, epoch 1 at 0.5×ttt_lr=0.0025): zero GPU overhead, prevents oscillation in the second pass, should improve per-chunk convergence.

Dynamic batch-size curriculum: use half-batch (393K tokens/step) for the first 20% of wallclock to get ~2x more gradient update steps during rapid early learning, then scale up to full batch (786K tokens/step) for stable late-stage refinement.
Bump ema_decay 0.998 → 0.9985 to align EMA timescale τ with the ~0.21·total-steps sweet spot from recent EMA-iterate-averaging literature, given our 72% linear warmdown.

Keep cosine warmdown shape but lower min_lr from 0.02 to 0.004, so the cosine's flat convergence plateau forms at 0.4% of peak LR instead of 2%, giving a tighter EMA convergence at the end of training.

Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping from 0.01→0.005 (halves regularization bias for sharper inverse Hessian) and explicitly pass block_size=32 (finer error propagation, restoring INIT.md baseline block size) — both structural GPTQ quality improvements.

Add a zero-init trigram channel (per-feature [dim] scale over the 2-prev-token normalized embedding) extending the existing bigram mechanism, giving the embedding pipeline a 2-hop causal look-back before the first transformer block.

Replace focal-loss weighting (gamma=0.5 on p_true) with MiLe-style top-64 entropy weighting: upweights tokens where the model is uncertain across multiple valid continuations, focusing gradient on inherently ambiguous positions rather than tokens the model is simply wrong about.

Warmdown-scheduled stochastic depth: decay the stochastic depth scale linearly from 1.0 to 0.0 during warmdown (72% of training), so the model gets clean full-depth gradient signals during convergence rather than noisy dropped-layer updates.

Layer-selective TTT: freeze tok_emb and blocks 0-4 during TTT, adapt only blocks 5-10 — fewer adaptable params reduces AllReduce volume on 8 GPUs and focuses gradient signal on task-specific late layers, within the 2-epoch budget.
Add Canon-A (kernel-4 depthwise conv, post-attn-norm pre-attn) per Allen-Zhu NeurIPS 2025 — Canon positions are cumulative, so A complements existing C.

Polyak EMA (decay=0.998) restricted to output-projection weights only (_g_out: attn.proj + mlp.proj, 22 tensors / ~58 MB) to reduce swap overhead from ~28s to ~1s while preserving smoothing benefit where it matters most — high-LR (3x) params with most gradient noise.

Raise TTT SGD momentum 0.9→0.95: with cosine LR decay across ~300 chunks the schedule front-loads adaptation; widening the momentum window (eff. 10→20 steps) preserves early-chunk gradient direction into late chunks where LR is near-zero, modestly improving cumulative adaptation.

Dynamic batch-size curriculum (fixed): half-batch (393K tok/step) for first 20% of wallclock for ~2x more gradient update steps early, then full batch (786K tok/step); warmup uses full batch to match baseline compile shape and avoid upward-size recompile crash.
Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping factor from 0.01 to 0.005 (halved regularization bias, sharper inverse Hessian), keeping block_size=64 to avoid eval time overrun.
Raise min_lr warmdown floor from 0.004 to 0.10 so late-training EMA steps see 10% of peak LR instead of 0.4%, keeping updates meaningful throughout the long warmdown_frac=0.72 decay phase.

Add label smoothing eps=0.1 to the CE loss: redistributes 10% of target mass uniformly, which per neural-collapse literature (ICLR 2025) accelerates convergence to well-separated feature representations—valuable in the severely undertrained 600s budget.
Add Canon-D (kernel-4 causal depthwise conv) inside MLP between fc and ReLU² activation, complementing existing Canon-A/Canon-C per Allen-Zhu NeurIPS 2025 cumulative-position ablation.
MiLe-style top-64 entropy weighting during training only (guard with self.training); TTT falls back to cheap focal weighting to avoid topk overhead across ~6000 TTT adaptation forward calls.
Add bigram×trigram element-wise interaction channel at input: captures the joint (t-2,t-1) token-pair signal that additive channels miss, using only a zero-init [dim] scale with negligible size cost.

Add Canon-B (depthwise kernel-4 conv on cat([Q,K,V]) pre-reshape) to complete the Canon A/B/C/D set per Allen-Zhu PhysicsLM4 (arXiv:2512.17351), which reports cumulative gain across positions; zero-init residual → step-0 identity, ~88 KB fp16 artifact cost.
Add mlp_global_drop=0.02 MLP activation dropout to all non-looped layers (0-2, 6-10), giving uniform light regularization across all 11 blocks instead of dropout only on looped layers 3-5.
Skip redundant eval_val_sliding when TTT is enabled (frees ~264s of budget) and invest it in 4 full-model TTT epochs instead of 2, restoring the SOTA-stack epoch count within the eval budget.
Extend TTT Polyak averaging from output projections only (_g_out) to all model parameters, giving more stable scored logits at every TTT chunk by averaging the full adapted model state.

Bump tied_embed_init_std from 0.005 → 0.010 to halve embedding warm-up wallclock and improve early gradient signal through the tied logit projection (init scale is not in the crowded beta/eps/wd/warmdown_frac LESSONS list).

Canon-B retry: insert depthwise kernel-4 conv on each of Q/K/V projection outputs (3 separate Canon modules) before reshape — mathematically identical to canon(cat([Q,K,V])) since Canon is per-channel, but avoids Tensor.split SymInt issues under torch.compile that may have caused the prior crash; same paper rationale (Allen-Zhu PhysicsLM4 cumulative A/B/C/D), zero-init step-0 identity.
Reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32 so quantization errors are propagated more frequently within each column block, improving weight reconstruction fidelity at no artifact-size cost.

Replace continuous EMA with LAWA (uniform average of ~50 snapshots at 1.45% intervals during the warmdown phase), focusing weight averaging on the fully-converged warmdown region instead of geometrically discounting all-of-training history.

Increase focal loss gamma from 0.5 to 1.0: sharpens focus on hard tokens (hard/easy weight ratio ~9x vs ~3x), concentrating more gradient signal on genuinely difficult tokens without any eval-time overhead.

Pre-compile the half-batch shape during warmup (2 extra steps before state reset) so the batch-curriculum switch at training step 0 hits the torch.compile cache instead of triggering a mid-loop recompile that counts against the MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS training budget, recovering ~60s of effective training time.
Add per-head learnable XSA subtraction strength (xsa_lambda init=1.0) so heads where exclusive-self-attention hurts can dial it down while default-1 reproduces Apple XSA exactly at step-0.
Skip eval_val_sliding when TTT enabled (saves ~28s) + ttt_epochs=3 (adds 1 epoch at ~27s cost): net ~+1s vs baseline — more adaptation passes at same wall-clock budget.

Add a learnable per-token output logit bias [vocab_size] initialized to zeros, applied before softcap, so the model can explicitly learn the unigram frequency prior and decouple it from contextual embedding representations.

Late Muon weight-decay taper: linearly reduce Muon WD from 100% to 50% of original starting at frac=0.70 of training, coinciding with deep warmdown phase — extracted from PR #1729 which showed +0.0042 BPB improvement.

Add parameter-free Peri-LN-style post-sublayer F.rms_norm on attn_out and mlp_out before attn_scale/mlp_scale multiply, stabilising the residual stream across the 11-layer + 3-layer-recurrence stack (per arXiv:2502.02732 / 2503.04598 HybridNorm).
Halve the GPTQ Hessian damping factor from 0.01 to 0.005 so quantization error correction is more faithful to the true curvature, improving weight reconstruction quality with zero compute overhead.

EMA_DECAY 0.9985 -> 0.999 to better match the long linear-to-zero warmdown (warmdown_frac=0.72) so the explicit EMA does not saturate before training ends and late-training (low-LR, low-noise) weights retain more weight in the final average.
Enable Nesterov momentum in TTT SGD and soften cosine LR endpoint from 25% to 50% of max, so each epoch adapts more meaningfully.

Focal gamma 0.5→1.0 (sharper focus on hard tokens) + ttt_epochs 3→2 (fix persistent DQ_EVAL: this environment consistently runs eval at ~603s vs baseline 575-595s, per INIT.md guidance to trim ttt_epochs).

Fix optimizer phase-alignment: restore warmdown_frac 0.72→0.58 and muon_momentum_warmup_fraction 0.33→0.22, so momentum warmup (ends at 22%) finishes well before LR warmdown starts (42%), giving a proper 20% stable phase instead of the current 5%-overlap where LR decay and momentum ramp are concurrent.

Revert per-block attn_norm/mlp_norm from DyT (alpha-only tanh, missing per-channel gamma) back to parameter-free RMSNorm — at our 11×512 scale the gamma-stripped DyT saturates useful signal that the post-sublayer scales cannot recover.

Per-chunk optimizer state reset (optimizer.state.clear()) plus cosine LR decay across TTT epochs (5→0.005 to 5→0.0005), so each chunk adapts from a clean momentum state with coarse-to-fine refinement instead of carrying stale cross-chunk momentum.

Replace uniform rope_dims=16 with a depth-aware 3-tier partial-RoPE schedule [24]*3+[16]*5+[8]*3 — early layers more rotated dims for positional/syntactic work, late layers larger NoPE region for content/semantic abstraction, run-mean preserved at 16.

Replace hard loss mask at seq_len//4 with a soft positional curriculum: keep primary loss over positions [512, 2048) and add a 0.1-weighted auxiliary loss over the previously masked prefix [0, 512). This provides gradient signal from short-context positions while high-context positions still dominate.
Reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32 for 2x more frequent inter-block error propagation, improving quantization fidelity especially for small models.

Add curr_bigram_scale [512, zero-init]: element-wise product of current and prev normalized embeddings injected into the input embedding, capturing the (current, prev) token-pair feature interaction missing from existing (prev, 2-prev) interaction_emb.

Couple MLP activation dropout to the existing stoch_depth_scale warmdown buffer so that dropout (mlp_loop_drop=0.05, mlp_global_drop=0.02) decays from full rate to zero in tandem with stochastic depth during the 72% warmdown phase, allowing clean final convergence without regularization noise.
Increase ttt_chunk_tokens from 32768 to 49152 (1.5x) — LaCT-style large-chunk score-first TTT for more per-step gradient diversity at near-iso-compute
Enable Nesterov momentum (nesterov=True) in the TTT SGD optimizer so each gradient step uses the lookahead momentum point, improving convergence quality within the fixed epoch budget.

Add NormFormer-style parameter-free F.rms_norm before both attention's output proj (post-h_gate, full-dim) and MLP's output proj (post-RELU²), bounding proj-input distributions to unit RMS for sharper GPTQ-int6 calibration; zero artifact cost.

Remove focal loss weighting (gamma=1.0) from training CE, keeping position ramp and z-loss; MiLe paper ablation shows gamma=0 (plain CE) achieves lower raw perplexity than gamma=1 focal since hard-token gradient bias directly hurts the BPB metric.

Replace cosine warmdown (min_lr=0.10) with linear warmdown to zero (min_lr=0.0), following arxiv 2502.15938 which shows linear decay-to-zero consistently outperforms cosine for compute-optimal training.

Implement ttt_freeze_layers=5 in eval_val_ttt: freeze blocks 0–4 (requires_grad=False), only adapt blocks 5–10 + embeddings + final norm, reducing backward-pass cost and focusing adaptation on abstract upper layers.
Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping factor from 0.005 to 0.001 of mean diagonal for less biased inverse Hessian estimation and more accurate column-error propagation.

Replace MLP `leaky_relu(0.5).square()` with strict Primer-style `relu().square()` to restore ~50% MLP-hidden sparsity, tightening the proj-input distribution for sharper GPTQ-int6 SDClip scales on the dominant 1.05M-param/block mlp.proj matrix.

Lower ttt_momentum 0.95→0.90 to align the across-chunk SGD momentum-buffer time constant with the per-chunk cosine LR decay (reducing late-chunk over-correction from high-LR early-chunk gradients carried forward).

Couple token-noise probability decay to the warmdown schedule (via stoch_depth_scale), so that like stochastic-depth and MLP dropout, token noise decays to 0 during the LR warmdown phase instead of corrupting final convergence with random token substitutions.

Revert soft positional curriculum (loss_main + 0.1*loss_prefix) to uniform CE across all 2048 positions, restoring full gradient signal; the soft curriculum reduced effective gradient by ~22% and inflated loss magnitude ~10%, misaligning LR calibration relative to the seed stack.

Reduce TTT Polyak EMA decay from 0.998 to 0.99 so the scoring model tracks recent chunk adaptations ~5x faster, giving mid-validation scoring a model that is ~94% adapted (vs ~42% with 0.998) and letting earlier chunks benefit from prior adaptation signal sooner.

Add HeadScaleLayer (per-feature scale, init=1) between final_norm and tied-embedding logit projection, so the model can independently up/down-weight embedding dimensions for input representation vs. logit output; also updates GPTQ Hessian hook to see the correct (scaled) activation, improving quantization fidelity.

Augment static per-head sigmoid output gate with input-dependent dynamic delta from a (num_heads, 12)-channel projection of the first 12 residual-stream dims (modded-nanogpt PR #117 / arXiv:2505.06708 Gated Attention NeurIPS 2025). Zero-init weight → step-0 identity vs current best; combined logit sigmoided once for context-based per-head no-op gating, alleviating BOS-sink dependence.

Remove z-loss: softcap=30 already bounds logit scale making z-loss redundant, and its gradient directly opposes CE on the correct token (pushing the correct logit down while CE pushes it up), wasting gradient capacity and adding ~0.02 nats of irremovable overhead.

Set min_lr=0.10 to floor LR warmdown at 10% of peak, preventing near-zero gradient updates in the final 6% of LAWA averaging window and sustaining useful gradient signal across all warmdown snapshots.

Replace SGD with AdamW (lr=5e-4, wd=0, betas=(0.9,0.999)) in the TTT inner loop; AdamW's per-parameter adaptive gradient scaling should converge more efficiently than uniform-LR SGD on the quantized model's heterogeneous gradient magnitudes across layers within the same 3-epoch budget.

Reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32 for twice-as-frequent cross-block Hessian error compensation, targeting the wide 2048-column MLP projection matrices where accumulated quantization error between corrections is highest.

Remove Canon-B (per-Q/K/V depthwise convs) inside attention — the existing Canon-A + Canon-C + Canon-D + shift_mix stack already saturates the horizontal-context axis at our 30M undertrained scale, and Canon-B on raw post-projection Q/K/V double-dips with Canon-A on the same data path; expect cleaner FA3 input and freed gradient capacity.
Add 10%-of-peak floor to TTT cosine LR so the late-chunk tail keeps drift-correcting instead of freezing (current schedule drops below 10% of peak by ci≈80/100 and below 1% by ci≈95).
Replace independent per-token noise with DropBlock-style contiguous 8-token span masking at the same 2% average rate, forcing the model to use longer-range context within masked spans.
Stride annealing during warmdown (seq_len//2 → seq_len//4) plus Van der Corput base-2 phase selection for uniform shard-reset coverage, reducing train/eval stride gap as weights converge.

Freeze per-head/per-channel control gain scalars (attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, q_gain, skip_weights, skip_gates) during TTT so only weight matrices adapt — prevents tiny tensors from overfitting to local chunk distributions (replicates exp_491 mechanism on new stack).
Add fourgram (t-3) embedding channel with zero-init per-feature scale, and initialize logit_bias from SentencePiece piece scores as a unigram prior for faster early convergence.
Reduce min_lr from 0.10 to 0.0 (linear decay-to-zero), motivated by arxiv:2502.15938 showing D2Z consistently outperforms linear decay to 10% — the exact schedule we run currently.
Raise attn_clip_sigmas from 10.0 to 12.0 so attention matrices (Q, K, V, O) use the same loose SDClip as MLP, preserving high-magnitude outliers in Q/K weights amplified by the QK-gain (5.25 init).
Set min_lr=0.1 (Chinchilla-style 10x decay floor) so the final ~7.2% of warmdown plateaus at 10% base LR instead of decaying to zero — under our undertrained 600s budget the D2Z tail wastes signal.

Remove Canon-D from MLP: inside-MLP kernel-4 depthwise conv on wide hidden=2048 has 8192 zero-init fp32 taps per layer that struggle to populate in our 6000-step undertrained regime; canon_a/canon_c on dim=512 already provide horizontal-context flow at 4x higher per-tap gradient flux. Following Canon-B removal precedent.

Switch TTT optimizer from AdamW to SGD with Nesterov momentum (momentum=0.9, lr=0.005) — cold-started AdamW is near-sign-gradient in the 3-step per-chunk regime, while Nesterov SGD takes full-size steps from step 0 with the lookahead gradient, matching effective step sizes and converging faster in the short adaptation window.

Anneal warmdown training stride aggressively from 1024 to 64 (matching eval stride=64) instead of stopping at 512, using slope (1-wd_prog) instead of (1-0.5*wd_prog), to close the train/eval stride distribution gap during model convergence.

Extend WD taper to token embedding AdamW optimizer (was only applied to Muon matrices): tied embeddings taper from embed_wd=0.085 to 0.0425 during final convergence (frac 0.70→1.00), letting the logit/embedding weights settle to a lower minimum without L2 restraint fighting final convergence.

Freeze control gain scalars (attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, q_gain, skip_weights, skip_gates) before torch.compile in TTT so dynamo sees final requires_grad state at trace time and never retraces mid-eval, fixing the eval_budget_overrun from the prior attempt.

Add fivegram_scale channel: pure normalized token embedding at t-4 (computed before any channel modifications), zero-initialized, giving the model an independent direct handle on 4-step-back context that is currently only accessible in entangled form inside the mixed fourgram channel.

Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown: lr = min_lr + (1-min_lr) * 0.5*(1+cos(π*t)), giving higher LR in early warmdown for more effective gradient steps and smoother convergence at the tail.
Reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32 so each block's Hessian error propagates at finer granularity, reducing accumulated quantization error per weight column.
Lower min_lr from 0.1 to 0.05 — halves the LR floor during the last ~7% of warmdown so the EMA endpoint absorbs less late-stage SGD noise.
Add gentle linear position weighting (0.5→1.5, mean=1.0) to training CE loss, aligning gradient emphasis with sliding-window eval where only the last stride (64/2048) positions are scored per window.
Restrict value-residual v_mix to top 4 layers (7-10) per ResFormer ACL'25 ablation; nullify v_mix on layers 0-6 where the paper reports v_first blending degrades early-layer generalization.

Apply Newton-Schulz gradient normalization (Muon-style) to 2D weight gradients in the TTT inner loop, replacing the clip_grad_norm_(1.0) that would otherwise nullify NS-normalized updates; this gives scale-invariant per-layer updates matching the LaCT paper finding that Muon outperforms momentum SGD for TTT.

Exclude control gain scalars (attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, q_gain, skip_weights, skip_gates) from TTT optimizer groups without changing requires_grad, so dynamo graph is identical to baseline (cache hit) but SGD never updates the tiny per-head/per-channel gains that overfit to chunk-local distributions.

Add per-layer attention output dropout (linear schedule 0→2% across layers, warmdown-coupled via stoch_depth_scale) as a complementary regularizer to the existing MLP dropout — a mechanism swap from 0→nonzero on the attention pathway.

Replace VDC base-2 phase with golden-ratio sequence (optimal 1D coverage) and switch warmdown stride annealing from linear to cosine (keeps larger/diverse stride early in warmdown when LR is still high, then rapidly converges to eval stride=64 at the end).

Add post-blend RMSNorm before x0 = x to normalize the full multi-channel embedding (bigram/trigram/fourgram/fivegram/boundary) to unit scale, giving every block's resid_mix injection a consistent skip-connection baseline.

Revert block_size to 64 (fixing timing issue from prior trial) and halve GPTQ Hessian damping from 0.001 to 0.0005, reducing regularization bias in the Cholesky inverse for more accurate weight compensation.
Bump tied_embed_init_std 0.010 → 0.014 to reduce flat-logit suppression of early embedding learning under tied weights.

Add Muon+ post-NS col-row normalization: after Newton-Schulz, normalize columns then rows (Muon+ arxiv 2602.21545), keeping existing pre-NS row-norm (MuonEq-R). Balances both input-feature and output-neuron gradient scales post-orthogonalization.

Add PaLM-style z-loss (weight=1e-4): z_loss = 1e-4 * mean(logsumexp(logits)^2) encourages moderate logit magnitudes and better-calibrated distributions, complementing existing label_smoothing=0.1 even under logit softcap.

Remove optimizer.state.clear() between TTT chunks so Nesterov SGD momentum warm-starts each chunk from the previous chunk's accumulated gradient direction; FineWeb validation is sequential text so consecutive 32K chunks are correlated and the 0.9^3≈0.73 natural decay gives ~3 chunks of useful gradient memory with no extra compute.

Make XSA subtraction strength input-dependent: add per-layer zero-init (num_heads,12) projection from the first 12 residual channels of the post-norm attention input, summed with the existing per-head static xsa_lambda before the self-projection subtraction; mirrors the validated attn_gate dynamic-delta pattern, lets λ scale with attention-sink risk per token instead of collapsing to one per-head compromise.

Extend stride annealing to the full training run via two-stage cosine: pre-warmdown cosines stride from seq_len=2048 (zero overlap, max diversity) down to seq_len//2=1024, joining continuously the existing warmdown cosine from 1024 to 64, so the model progressively closes the training-eval distribution gap throughout all of training.

Extend WD taper window earlier (start_frac 0.70→0.55) and deepen it (final_mult 0.50→0.15) so weight decay tapers to near-zero during the dominant warmdown convergence phase, allowing tighter gradient-direction fitting when LR is still meaningful.

Change polyak_decay in eval_val_ttt from 0.99 to 0.9 so the Polyak-averaged scoring model incorporates ~27% of TTT-adapted weights per chunk (vs ~3% at 0.99), meaningfully exploiting TTT adaptation in early chunks where the EMA was previously indistinguishable from the initial model.
Reduce GPTQ block_size from 64 to 32, enabling more frequent Hessian-error-propagation passes per weight matrix and tighter quantization residuals without affecting artifact size or training.
Set MIN_LR floor 0.05 → 0.0 (D2Z, arxiv 2502.15938) so the linear warmdown decays straight to zero in the final ~3.6% of wallclock instead of being held at a 5% floor.

Add trigram×fourgram interaction channel (t-3, t-2 token pair element-wise product) to the embedding pipeline, mirroring the existing bigram×trigram interaction but shifted one step back, giving the model explicit access to the (t-3,t-2) co-occurrence pattern at the input layer.

Denser LAWA sampling (interval 0.0145→0.006, ~2.5× more snapshots during warmdown) plus momentum warmdown coupling (Muon momentum ramps 0.99→0.995 as LR cosine-decays) for more stable low-LR convergence.
Reduce label_smoothing from 0.1 to 0.05 to reduce over-regularization: allows the model to target more confident correct predictions during warmdown, better aligning training loss with eval NLL.

Switch TTT optimizer from SGD+Nesterov to AdamW(betas=(0.9,0.9), wd=0.01) to get per-parameter adaptive LR across heterogeneous layers with fast-converging second moments in the score-first sequential adaptation loop.
Pull parallel_residual_start from 7→9: layers 7,8 flip parallel→sequential, giving 2 more multi-step refinement layers right after recurrence; layers 9-10 stay parallel.
polyak_decay 0.99→0.9 (TTT scoring uses ~27% adapted vs ~3%) + gptq_calibration_batches 64→56 to save ~4-5s of GPTQ time and avoid the 3s eval overrun seen in the prior DQ_EVAL run.
block_size=32 (finer GPTQ error correction) + calibration_batches=40 (was 48) to recover eval time headroom after block_size reduction added ~4-8s to GPTQ.

Add trigram×fourgram interaction channel (t-3, t-2 token pair element-wise product) to the embedding pipeline — retry after marginal eval_budget_overrun of 3.5s which is within known environment-jitter range.

Shift wd_taper_start_frac from 0.55 to 0.40 to align WD tapering with the current warmdown schedule (warmdown_frac=0.72 → LR warmdown starts at 28%), maintaining the same ~12-14pp gap between warmdown start and WD taper start as the SOTA configuration, giving the undertrained model more gradient freedom during the bulk of convergence.
Lower ttt_momentum from 0.90 → 0.85 to better fit per-chunk SGD horizon (~9 steps/rank) and reduce cross-chunk gradient memory under per-chunk distribution shift.

Decouple stride warmdown from LR warmdown: new sampler_warmdown_frac=0.80 sets stride warmdown_start=0.20, aligning it with the batch-curriculum transition instead of the LR warmdown start (0.28), so both phases of refinement begin together.

Long-short SWA: layers 0,1,2,6,7,8 use FA3 causal-local window=512; recurrence (3-5) and parallel-residual (9-10) layers stay full attention — frees ~25-30% of attention compute per forward, translating into more training steps under the 600s budget while preserving global attention where it matters most.

Start LAWA at the midpoint of the warmdown phase (frac=0.71) instead of at the beginning (frac=0.42), so the weight average covers only the lower-LR, more-converged tail of warmdown rather than the full noisy warmdown period.

Scheduled label smoothing: anneal the training label-smoothing from 0.05 to 0 linearly over the last 20% of training (frac 0.80→1.0), implemented via an nn.Buffer so torch.compile reads it dynamically; at EMA-snapshot time _ls≈0.001 meaning TTT adapts with near-pure NLL, tightening the train/eval objective gap.

Reduce ttt_freeze_layers from 5 to 3 (SGD TTT unchanged), adapting the depth-recurrent looped blocks 3-4 which run 3x per forward pass and accumulate 3x more gradient signal per parameter, while reverting the failed AdamW change.

Extend batch-curriculum half-batch phase from 20% to 70% of wallclock, based on 'Fast Catch-Up, Late Switching' theory (arxiv 2602.14208) that hard/undertrained tasks benefit from small batches for most of training with a late switch to large batch.

Fix fourgram channel consistency: compute fourgram_emb from pure RMSNorm'd embeddings (before embed_scale), making it consistent with all other n-gram channels (bigram, trigram, fivegram) which all use the pre-embed_scale normalized embedding.

Lower ema_decay 0.999→0.997 (half-life ~693→~230 steps) to remove ~9% random-init contamination of the post-training EMA checkpoint and confine averaging to the decay phase where the D2Z basin is actually converged.
Skip redundant eval_val_sliding compile+scoring when TTT is enabled (saves ~15-25s eval budget), then use freed budget to restore ttt_epochs from 3→4 for better per-chunk TTT adaptation.

Extend WD taper to the scalar optimizer (q_gain, resid_mix, attn_scale, mlp_scale, skip_weights, skip_gates) which previously had constant adam_wd=0.02 throughout training — aligning WD decay with LR decay during warmdown so architectural control params can settle without being pulled toward zero.
Reduce SDClip sigmas for MLP (12.85→9.5) and attention (12.0→9.0) to improve int6 quantization resolution by ~38% without real clipping, since 9+σ still covers essentially all weights in practice

Replace cosine warmdown (min_lr=0.10) with linear warmdown (min_lr=0.02): per arXiv 2502.15938, linear decay to near-zero outperforms cosine by maintaining higher LR later in warmdown and allowing fuller convergence.

Long-short SWA: layers 0,1,2,6,7,8 use FA3 causal-local window=512; recurrence (3-5) and parallel-residual (9-10) layers stay full attention — frees ~25-30% of attention compute per forward, translating into more training steps under the 600s budget while preserving global attention where it matters most.

Remove label_smoothing entirely (0.05→0.0) from F.cross_entropy: pure NLL training aligns the training objective with the sliding-window and TTT eval metrics, and the logit softcap + EMA provide sufficient implicit regularisation.
Replace standard cross-entropy with focal loss (gamma=2) in TTT adaptation step, upweighting high-perplexity tokens to concentrate gradient signal where adaptation is most needed.

Fix fourgram channel to use pure normalized embeddings (consistent with other n-gram channels) + reduce gptq_calibration_batches 64→54 to save ~6s eval time and stay within the 600s eval budget in this environment.

Extend batch-curriculum half-batch phase from 20% to 35% of wallclock (aligned to enable_looping_at), covering the entire constant-LR period plus a short early-warmdown slice for more gradient steps before depth recurrence activates.
Skip torch.compile(eval_model) + eval_val('quantized') + eval_val_sliding when TTT enabled (saves ~25s additional on top of prior run), enabling ttt_epochs=4 within the 600s eval budget.

Bump tied_embed_init_std from 0.014 to 0.020 (GPT-2 default) so rare-token embedding rows have more distinguishable representations from step 0 (improves long-tail BPB under short ~2400-step training) and widens int8-GPTQ dynamic range on the tied output projection.

Replace flat cross-entropy with focal-weighted NLL (gamma=0.5): tokens where the model is still uncertain receive near-full gradient, high-confidence tokens are gently down-weighted, concentrating learning capacity on rare/hard tokens that dominate BPB.

Focal-weighted NLL (gamma=0.5) via log_softmax+gather+detach (fullgraph-compile-safe): uncertain tokens receive near-full gradient, confident tokens are gently down-weighted, concentrating learning on rare/hard tokens that dominate BPB.
Set min_lr=0.0 (linear decay-to-zero) instead of 0.02, following arxiv 2502.15938 which shows D2Z consistently outperforms partial-decay schedules by allowing full LR annealing to zero.
Use float64 for GPTQ Hessian Cholesky factorization instead of float32, improving numerical stability for ill-conditioned Hessians (condition number 1e4+) without changing artifact size or format

Mechanism swap: replace weak mlp_global_drop=0.02 (activation noise in non-looped MLP layers) with embed_noise_std=0.03 (continuous Gaussian perturbation at normalized embedding level, warmdown-coupled via stoch_depth_scale).

Reshuffle layer-wise RoPE schedule from [24,24,24,16,16,16,16,16,8,8,8] to [24,24,24,8,8,8,28,28,8,8,8] — make 3-layer recurrence zone (layers 3-5) NoPE-dominant for iterative content-alignment, concentrate freed RoPE budget on layers 6-7 (post-recurrence re-anchoring with SWA-512). Mean rope_dims preserved at 16, zero artifact-byte cost.

Extend half-batch curriculum from frac=0.35 to frac=0.65 (decoupled from looping activation), adding ~30% more training time with 2x gradient steps; pre-compile half-batch+looping graph during warmup to avoid mid-training recompile penalty.

Add Universal-Transformer per-iteration step embedding to the 3-layer recurrence loop: (num_loops, model_dim) zero-init learnable additive shift injected at the start of each non-first iteration to break the symmetry that currently forces all 3 passes through blocks 3-5 to compute identically modulo input.

Replace TTT optimizer from SGD+Nesterov (lr=0.005) to AdamW (lr=0.001, wd=0.0, betas=(0.9,0.999)) to get per-parameter adaptive LR scaling that accumulates cross-chunk importance weighting via the second moment estimate.
Revert ttt_epochs to 3 (4 was marginally worse) and reduce eval_stride from 64 to 48, using the 55s headroom revealed by the eval-optimization to score 33% more windows with longer per-token context.

Bump scalar_lr 0.02 → 0.030 (1.36× matrix_lr) so dense-gradient 1D modulators (q_gain, attn/mlp scales, skip gates, resid_mix) adapt faster within the ~2400-step budget — Muon-doc convention places scalar AdamW LR above orthogonalized matrix LR.
